events.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
events.summary:
Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe

Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues. In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared a heat hazard, the first race to do so this season. Temperatures are expected to rise into the low 30s celsius during the race, almost 10C warmer than usual for the venue.

Across Europe, the intense heat has led to severe thunderstorms. In the Sverdlovsk region of central Russia, a strong tornado was reported on the evening of 22 June. Rated a 3 out of 5 on the international Fujita scale, the twister injured 16 people and destroyed or damaged about 100 homes and businesses in the town of Kushva. Emergency services have been working on restoring power to the town. Tornadoes are not unheard of in Russia but are rare. Forecasters say they may become more common in future as the climate breaks down. Storm warnings remained in effect in the southern part of the country on Friday.

Governments appear to be listening too. This month the UK became the latest country to state that it would set a minimum age of 16 for accessing major social media platforms. events.title: Donald Trump threatens to annihilate Iran after crossfire over Hormuz – as it happened
events.summary:
Iran attacked Bahrain and Kuwait after US strikes, and threatened a ‘complete halt’ to talks

  • US and Iran trade strikes as both sides accuse the other of endangering ceasefire

We will soon be closing this liveblog, but you’ll be able to stay up-to-date with our ongoing coverage of the Middle East here.

Here is a summary of today’s events:

Iran launched drone and missile attacks Sunday targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic, and threatened a “complete halt” in negotiations to end the war if Washington continues its attacks.

US president Donald Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire agreement in a post of social media and said the US may be “forced to militarily complete the job”. Iran also accused the US of violating the ceasefire agreement.

JD Vance continued to reiterate the administration’s triumphant line on the war with Iran hours before the latest round of strikes were exchanged. “America wins either way,” he said.

Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Araghchi was in Baghdad for a meeting with his Iraqi counterpart. He called for a security framework to be established with the Gulf nations after it struck US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain in retaliation to US strikes.

The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps IRCG has said on state-run SNN TV that it will respond with more force if there are any more blow-for-blow attacks from the US.

Countries including Jordan, the UAE and Italy all condemned Iran’s attacks.

Continue reading...

events.title: Escalating US-Iran strikes threaten interim peace agreement
events.summary:
Tehran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait amid efforts to open strait of Hormuz without Iran’s direct oversight

  • Fresh hostilities in Gulf suggest US-Iran memorandum was too broadly worded

A new round of escalating strikes between Iran and the US has continued, further undermining the fragile interim peace agreement between the two countries, and prompting Donald Trump to threaten violence that would ensure Iran “will no longer exist”.

On Sunday, Tehran launched drone and missile attacks against Bahrain and Kuwait after new US strikes on sites in southern Iran, and threatened a “complete halt” to negotiations to end the war. Trump said that a moment might come soon when he abandoned talks and the US would “militarily finish the job”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Shadow war: how use of proxy forces by Iran, Israel and US is driving Middle East instability
events.summary:
Across region there is a push to disarm militia and reinforce state authority but the temptation to use proxies remains

As Marco Rubio ended his brief visit to the Middle East on Friday, he sought to cast in the best possible light his discussions with leaders of the Gulf states. Those leaders are deeply anxious that the deal agreed earlier this month between Iran and the US fails to address their worries about continued Iranian efforts to project power and influence throughout the region.

“They’ve shared with us some very concrete concerns,” the US secretary of state admitted, and insisted that any definitive agreement will require Tehran to not only restrict its nuclear programme but also halt its support of Hamas in Gaza, Hezbollah in Lebanon, militia in Iraq and the Houthis in Yemen.

Continue reading...

events.title: Bahrain condemns Iranian tit-for-tat drone attack as ‘flagrant threat’
events.summary:
No immediate reports of damage after Bahrain hit by ‘number of drones’ as ship in strait of Hormuz also targeted

Bahrain has said it was attacked by Iran with drones on Saturday, apparently in response to overnight US strikes on Iran. A ship in the strait of Hormuz was also attacked.

Bahrain’s foreign ministry said a “number of drones” were launched at the country, though there were no immediate reports of damage. It condemned the attack and described it as a “flagrant threat to the security of citizens and residents”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds
events.summary:
Fears hard-won gains in reducing child mortality over 20 years are at risk after end of USAID funding for nutrition programmes

Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached “alarming” levels, according to the largest ever survey of under-fives in the country.

The new figures came just over a year after USAID, the former US flagship agency closed by the Trump administration in 2025, stopped funding work on child nutrition in Nepal.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
events.summary:
Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western province

Pakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.

The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said.

Continue reading...

events.title: EU faces fierce criticism over plans to host Taliban in Brussels
events.summary:
Rights campaigners and MEPs say meeting would normalise regime that erases women from public life

Rights campaigners and MEPs have warned that a meeting between EU officials and a Taliban delegation in Brussels risks normalising a regime that has banned girls from school beyond the sixth grade and sought to erase women from public life, while its ranks include two leaders accused of crimes against humanity.

A spokesperson for the Afghan foreign ministry confirmed that a delegation representing the ⁠Taliban had travelled to Brussels after the Belgian foreign ministry issued five single-day visas.

Continue reading...

events.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
events.summary:
Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition

The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions.

In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff” from using mobile phones.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
events.summary:
Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’

Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges.

Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remove the sales tax.

Continue reading...

events.title: London mayor to override opposition to outdoor dining in Soho next summer
events.summary:
Exclusive: Sadiq Khan plans to use new powers after local council opposes city scheme for seasonal pedestrianisation

The mayor of London, Sadiq Khan, plans to override opposition to al fresco dining in Soho after the local council failed to apply to a scheme for seasonal pedestrianisation that is being introduced across the capital this summer.

From 2027, restaurants in London’s entertainment district will be allowed to put chairs outside in the warmer months despite a longstanding local row on the issue.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘A privilege – and pretty terrifying’: James Norton to play Hamlet in the West End
events.summary:
The Happy Valley actor is lined up as the lead in German director Thomas Ostermeier’s first Shakespearean play in English

James Norton is to take on his first major Shakespearean stage role and play Hamlet in the West End next year.

The King & Conqueror star said it was a “privilege” and “pretty terrifying” to be cast as the tragic prince in the acclaimed German director Thomas Ostermeier’s production next autumn. Norton was last on stage in 2023 in the harrowing A Little Life, based on Hanya Yanagihara’s novel, which the actor described as the hardest thing he has ever done.

Continue reading...

events.title: About 170,000 people in England expected to die from obesity-linked heart conditions by 2035
events.summary:
British Heart Foundation expects about 45 people a day to die over next decade if current trends continue

About 170,000 people are expected to die from heart-related conditions linked to obesity – one of the leading causes of preventable illnesses – by 2035, according to a leading charity.

The analysis, conducted by the British Heart Foundation (BHF), found that about 45 people a day are expected to die from cardiovascular disease linked to excess weight and obesity in England over the next decade, as long as current trends in obesity rates continue. In the UK, about two in three adults are living with obesity, and worldwide, more than half of adults and a third of children and young people will be overweight or obese by 2050.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pause HS2 reset until you are confident it can be delivered, NAO tells ministers
events.summary:
Spending watchdog says high-speed rail project must be put on stable footing to avoid repeat of costly past failures

Revised plans for HS2 should not be put into action until the government is confident they can be delivered, according to the public spending watchdog.

The project to build the high-speed railway must be put on a stable footing to avoid a repeat of past failures, the National Audit Office (NAO) said in a report.

Continue reading...

events.title: England facing children’s mental health ‘crisis’ as referrals hit 1m
events.summary:
Commissioner calls for overhaul of state support after reporting 10% rise in young patient referrals last year

More than 1 million children were referred to mental health services across England last year, with referrals up 10% from the year previous, according to a report by the children’s commissioner, Rachel de Souza. She said the country faced a “crisis” in young people’s mental health.

The number of patients who had an active referral to children and young people’s mental health services across England surpassed 1 million for the first time in 2024-25. This was almost double the number recorded in 2018-19, and an almost 10% rise on the previous year.

Continue reading...

events.title: Heatwave and high humidity to blast much of US: ‘impactful to anyone’
events.summary:
Forecasters expect days of high temperatures in central and eastern US before Fourth of July and World Cup festivities

A long and dangerous heatwave will blast a large swath of the central and eastern United States for the upcoming week, the National Weather Service (NWS) said on Sunday – with temperatures rising ahead of the Fourth of July holiday and feeling even hotter because of high humidity also arriving.

Already parts of the US, especially Phoenix and central Texas, and much of the south-west were experiencing temperatures of about 100F (38C) on Sunday, while the NWS warned of severe wildfire conditions developing across much of the west as new fires popped up across the region.

Continue reading...

events.title: US homeland security secretary tells migrants to seek permanent status or leave
events.summary:
Markwayne Mullin’s remarks come after controversial supreme court ruling to strip TPS from over 350,000 people

Migrants in the US on temporary protected status should seek permanent residence or leave, Markwayne Mullin, Homeland Security secretary, said in the wake of last week’s supreme court decision that stripped humanitarian protections from hundreds of thousands of immigrants.

The remarks to CNN’s State of the Union program comes after a decision ⁠that could allow Donald Trump’s administration to deport Haitian and Syrian immigrants to home countries plagued by conflict and ​destitution.

Continue reading...

events.title: Zohran Mamdani says he and allies he endorsed carry a ‘national message’
events.summary:
Mayor says progressive peers who swept primaries speak to Americans ‘coast to coast’ as moderates have reservations

Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, said on Sunday that he and a slew of democratic socialist allies who prevailed in recent primary elections are carrying a “national message” to struggling working Americans hungry for a new kind of politics “coast to coast”.

Mamdani made that triumphant clarion call on ABC News’s This Week just five days after he had seen his endorsed candidates win Democratic nominations in three races for New York congressional seats, as well as for five state legislature positions in Albany. He made no effort to disguise his delight that his clean sweep marks a dramatic shift in Democratic politics – not just in New York City, which he has led since January, but also across the US.

Continue reading...

events.title: Three firefighters killed and two injured while battling Utah-Colorado wildfires
events.summary:
Firefighters were involved in joint response to Knowles and Gore blazes as wildfires ravage western US, fire agency says

Three firefighters were killed and two others were hurt while responding to wildfires along the Colorado-Utah border, the US Wildland Fire Service announced on Sunday.

The agency, which was established in January to coordinate wildfire suppression and prevention efforts across public lands, said the firefighters were involved in a joint response to the Knowles and Gore fires on Saturday.

Continue reading...

events.title: Bill Cassidy accuses Trump of treating Congress as ‘merely an appendage’
events.summary:
Out-going Louisiana senator’s rebuke over Iran war is rare instance of a Republican politician standing up to Trump

Bill Cassidy, the Republican senator from Louisiana who is being ousted from his position after Donald Trump successfully backed a challenger in May’s primary, has accused the US president of treating Congress as “merely an appendage” in his handling of the Iran war.

In an interview on Sunday with CBS News’s Face the Nation, the out-going Cassidy explained his recent face-to-face row with Trump over the president’s failure to brief Congress on the prosecution of the hostilities with Tehran. In a fleetingly rare instance of a Republican politician directly standing up to Trump, Cassidy let rip at a Capitol Hill lunch over the senator’s support for a war powers resolution that was a symbolic rebuke to the White House.

Continue reading...

events.title: In Venezuela, silence has become a rescue tool
events.summary: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.

events.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
events.summary: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Trade tensions shake up Brazil's caipirinha spirit
events.summary: U.S. tariff pressure is pushing Europe and Brazil closer—opening new global doors for everything from aircraft parts to Brazil's cachaça, the base of the caipirinha.

events.title: 'If you are alive, make any noise': Venezuela searches rubble on day 4
events.summary: Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.

events.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
events.summary: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.

events.title: New strikes test the Iran ceasefire
events.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.

events.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak
events.summary: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.

events.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
events.summary: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

events.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
events.summary: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.

events.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
events.summary: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

events.title: Serbia: Protests continue after Vucic says he will step down
events.summary: Student-led demonstrations continued even after President Vucic announced his plan to resign within weeks. For more than 18 months, demonstrators have kept Vucic under pressure.

events.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
events.summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

events.title: Germany's development aid strategy under fire
events.summary: Days ahead of the launch of the North-South Commission, development aid organizations have urged the German government to reverse billions in cuts to development aid.

events.title: Pakistan's Geo News apologizes for Muharram broadcast
events.summary: The broadcaster Geo News was handed a 15-day suspension for a documentary that was "liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers," Pakistan's media watchdog said.

events.title: Uganda military chief shuts media outlets, slams 'free press'
events.summary: Muhoozi Kainerugaba said that he issued the order as he does not "believe in a free press." He is the son of President Yoweri Museveni, who has held his grip on power for decades.

events.title: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
events.summary: The Russian leader condemned what he called "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukraine has targeted oil refineries and weapons factories, causing massive disruption in recent weeks.

events.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
events.summary: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

events.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crashes, all 14 passengers killed
events.summary: Saudi Arabian media report that the helicopter crashed in Ras Tanura on the country's eastern coast early on Sunday. It said that all 14 Saudi nationals on board were killed, and the cause of the crash was unclear.

events.title: France: 11 killed in civilian plane crash
events.summary: Police have urged people to "strictly avoid" the area around the airport in Tomblaine. The plane was reportedly carrying skydivers.

events.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
events.summary: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

events.title: With 'Operation Purgatory,' Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
events.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

events.title: New Caledonia heads to polls in vote delayed since 2024
events.summary: Security has been high at polling stations as the South Pacific archipelago holds its first provincial elections since 2019. The vote in the French territory was originally set for 2024 but was delayed by violent unrest.

events.title: Europe heat wave: Over 1,300 excess deaths, WHO says
events.summary: The WHO said over 1,300 excess deaths have been recorded in Europe since the record-breaking heat wave started around June 21. Germany logged yet another heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius.

events.title: Argentina: Milei's top aide resigns over corruption scandal
events.summary: Manuel Adorni is mired in a corruption scandal but has denied wrongdoing. Javier Milei has defended Adorni, saying, "Manuel is innocent."

events.title: Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind the protests?
events.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

events.title: Hungary holds first post-Orban Budapest Pride march
events.summary: Tens of thousands packed Budapest's streets despite scorching heat. Former right-wing leader Viktor Orban tried to ban the march last year as part of a wider LGBTQ+ crackdown.

events.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Nearly 7 million may be impacted — UN
events.summary: The projection includes up to two million people in the capital, Caracas. Rescue teams are racing against time to save survivors, three days after the twin quakes that killed 1,450 people. DW has more.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
events.summary: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

events.title: Why is the US targeting Germany's drug industry?
events.summary: A new trade probe is reigniting a long-running clash over who should pay for medical innovation. Washington argues Germans benefit from lower prices while US patients shoulder a disproportionate share of the costs.

events.title: Rome celebrates 80 years of Italy's iconic Vespa scooter
events.summary: Thousands of Vespa riders took to Rome's streets to mark Vespa's 80th birthday. The wasp-waisted scooter became a symbol of Italy's rebirth after World War II.

events.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
events.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
events.summary: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

events.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
events.summary: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: Fact check: Throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
events.summary: An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

events.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
events.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

events.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
events.summary: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

events.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
events.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
events.summary: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

events.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
events.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

events.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
events.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

events.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
events.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

events.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
events.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

events.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
events.summary: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

events.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
events.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
events.summary: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
events.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

events.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
events.summary: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

events.title: Climate change: Heat wave costs the German economy billions
events.summary: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: World Cup: Why Mexico may be LA's favorite team
events.summary: Mexican-American football fans in the USA often split allegiances between their two cultures. For many in Los Angeles, the Mexico national team trumps the nation they call home.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
events.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

events.title: Curacao's trailblazing doctor at the World Cup
events.summary: Suzanne Huurman is the only female chief doctor at the 2026 World Cup. Her work is inspiring change in men's football.

events.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
events.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

events.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
events.summary: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

events.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
events.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

events.title: Experts axed by Trump keep climate information flowing
events.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
events.summary: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

events.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
events.summary: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
events.summary: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
events.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

events.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
events.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

events.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
events.summary: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

events.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
events.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

events.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
events.summary: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

events.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
events.summary: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

events.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
events.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
events.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

events.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
events.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

events.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
events.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

events.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
events.summary: With three goals in two games, striker Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany at the World Cup. But things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.

events.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
events.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

events.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
events.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

events.title: Germany's media rocked by AI scandal
events.summary: Two German outlets were forced to delete articles that used undisclosed AI. Many fear that reliance on the technology will damage the credibility of German media.

events.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
events.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

events.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
events.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

events.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
events.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

events.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
events.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

events.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
events.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

events.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
events.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

events.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
events.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

events.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
events.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

events.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
events.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

events.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
events.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

events.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
events.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

events.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
events.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

events.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?
events.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

events.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
events.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

events.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

events.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
events.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

events.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
events.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

events.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
events.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

events.title: Baerbock: 'Germany team is an example to Germany's kids'
events.summary: The president of the UN General Assembly, Annalena Baerbock, praises the diversity of Germany's football team as it plays in the World Cup.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
events.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

events.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
events.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

events.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
events.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

events.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
events.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

events.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
events.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

events.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
events.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

events.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
events.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

events.title: Why the Williams sisters and other stars are playing longer
events.summary: More than three decades after they burst onto the tennis scene, Venus and Serena Williams are still playing at the top level. Why are we seeing elite athletes carrying on into their 40s and beyond?

events.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
events.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

events.title: World Cup dreams come true for young German footballers
events.summary: Three young US college soccer players from Germany have had the opportunity to meet some of their World Cup heroes, as the men's natonal team's base camp is just around the corner.

events.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes
events.summary: After starring in Cape Verde's draw with Spain, goalkeeper Vozinha shared his sadness that an expensive travel bond stopped his mother from attending. On the same day, Iran's coach said his team were being "oppressed."

events.title: Leipzig Bach festival: Encouraging dialog
events.summary: Talking, debating and listening are skills we can all learn, particularly in the light of current global conflicts. The Leipzig Bachfest shows how Johann Sebastian Bach's music can serve as a model here.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
events.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't end shipping risks
events.summary: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But naval mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

events.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
events.summary: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.

events.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
events.summary: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: Manuel Neuer: The aura of a record-breaking goalkeeper
events.summary: Germany's hopes at the 2026 World Cup are connected to world-class goalkeeper Manuel Neuer's fitness and form.

events.title: Germany start 2026 World Cup with win over Curacao
events.summary: For the first time since they won the tournament in 2014, Germany won their opening game at the World Cup.

events.title: Fact check: These FIFA World Cup ticket sites are fake
events.summary: Desperate to get hold of tickets for the football World Cup, many fans face being scammed by sites set up to sell fake tickets. Here's how to make sure you don't fall into the trap.

events.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
events.summary: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
events.summary: SpaceX floated on the Nasdaq stock exchange on Friday, with shares trading 25% above the opening price at one point. The IPO has made Musk the world's first trillionaire, at least on paper.

events.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
events.summary: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather bag fails to sell at auction
events.summary: The handbag promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product" didn't find a buyer at auction. Is it more chicken than dinosaur?

events.title: Preaching and teaching in a warming world
events.summary: US pastors, a Ugandan musician and salmon-friendly tech delivered good environmental news this week.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.summary: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.summary: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Sex doll art sparks debate at German president's residence
events.summary: Beyond a headline-grabbing bronze, contemporary artworks draw attention to political power at an exhibition held at Bellevue Palace before the state building closes for renovation.

events.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
events.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

events.title: The climate conversation many US pastors avoid
events.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental protections, some Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land. But many others stay silent.

events.title: Leipzig Bachfest 2026: Bach's music as hit parade
events.summary: The Leipzig Bach Festival honors the composer with a hit parade. Over 8,000 fans from 20 countries cast their votes. But Bach is celebrated in many other countries around the world as well.

events.title: Why Anthropic has the edge over OpenAI in IPO race
events.summary: The rivalry between Sam Altman and Dario Amodei is entering a new phase. Both AI bosses are planning public listings and hope to use the money to outpace each other in the race to build superintelligent AI.

events.title: Depression in romantic relationships: You, me and the illness
events.summary: Depression can be a huge challenge for a relationship. Loved ones of someone with depression often feel helpless. But there is actually a lot they can do ― and a lot they shouldn't.

events.title: Jafar Panahi's prison sentence confirmed by Iranian court
events.summary: Following a retrial for a verdict that had been reached while the prominent Iranian director was abroad, the Revolutionary Court in Tehran has upheld a one-year jail sentence.

events.title: High-functioning depression: When success masks suffering
events.summary: People with depression don't always feel overwhelmed or struggle to get things done — some are highly efficient and productive, despite their low moods. So it can't be that bad, right? Wrong!

events.title: Why businesses are offering 'Quiet Hour'
events.summary: More peace and quiet, less stress — the Quiet Hour is an initiative for people with invisible disabilities. An increasing number of stores and institutions in Germany are launching similar campaigns.

events.title: Desalination hopes and reformed poachers
events.summary: EVs surge in Ethiopia, former poachers offer seahorse tours and India's ghost villages are repopulated.

events.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
events.summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

events.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
events.summary: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

events.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
events.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

events.title: Can Hawaii wean itself off oil imports?
events.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

events.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
events.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

events.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
events.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
events.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

events.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
events.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

events.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
events.summary: Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time since 2019. Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

events.title: German town prints its own cash to cut CO2 emissions
events.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
events.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
events.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

events.title: Iran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait, threatens to halt talks after fresh US strikes
events.summary: Iran launched fresh drone and missile attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait on Sunday after new US airstrikes, while warning it would completely halt negotiations and close the Strait of Hormuz if Washington continues its attacks.

events.title: Armed conflict threatens Ethiopia’s ancient monasteries
events.summary: Lake Tana in Ethiopia’s Amhara region was once a tourist hotspot, known for its picturesque shorelines and monasteries dating back to the 14th century. But since 2023, an ongoing war between the Fano militias and federal government forces has scared away the tourists. Cities are subject to shootings and grenade attacks, while rural areas face drone strikes and kidnappings for ransom. Now, the future of these ancient monasteries is uncertain. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

events.title: Canada reach last 16 with stoppage-time win over South Africa
events.summary: Stephen Eustaquio struck deep into stoppage time to give Canada a dramatic 1-0 victory over South Africa on Sunday, sending the World Cup co-hosts into the last 16 for the first time in their history. Canada will face either the Netherlands or Morocco in Houston on July 4.

events.title: Turkey police detain dozens at LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul
events.summary: Turkish police on Sunday detained at least 50 people, including a journalist, during a LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul that went ahead despite a ban by local authorities. Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but since 2015, the annual Pride march has been almost systematically banned and suppressed.

events.title: New Caledonia votes: High stakes in France's Pacific territory
events.summary: New Caledonia voted Sunday in local elections that are key to determining the French Pacific territory’s political future and relationship with France. FRANCE 24’s Leela Jacinto explains the stakes and how decolonization and demographics play critical roles in France’s Pacific foothold.

events.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board
events.summary: A skydiving plane crashed in eastern France on Sunday, killing all 11 people on board. The crash in the town of Tomblaine killed five instructors, five students and the pilot, according to local officials. The cause of the incident was not immediately clear, and a technical investigation has been opened, according to a French deputy public prosecutor.

events.title: Ukrainian strike ignites Russian oil refinery, as Putin acknowledges 'difficult period'
events.summary: Debris from downed Ukrainian drones sparked a blaze at a major oil refinery in Russia on Sunday, according to local authorities. This comes as Russian President Vladimir Putin said that his country was “going through a difficult period”. In recent months, Ukraine has stepped up its long-range attacks on Russian military facilities, choking Moscow's fuel supplies and military deliveries.

events.title: New Caledonia: Provisional results show anti-independence parties lead
events.summary: New Caledonia's provincial elections are a crucial test of the territory's political future, with independence from France remaining the central issue. The possibility of independence from France remains the central political issue, with today’s vote taking place peacefully. The election comes two years after plans to expand voting rights to thousands of non-Indigenous residents triggered deadly unrest and forced the postponement of elections in 2024. France 24 International affairs editor Leela Jacinto gives further insights.

events.title: Tehran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after US attacks
events.summary: The ceasefire between Iran and the United States appears increasingly fragile on Sunday, following a new exchange of strikes. In the latest escalation, Tehran launched missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait, both of which host US military bases, just hours after the United States struck multiple targets across Iran. The renewed violence comes amid rising tensions over efforts to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route.

events.title: Rangnick dismisses collusion talk after Austria and Algeria advance after ‘mad’ World Cup draw
events.summary: Austria coach Ralf Rangnick dismissed suggestions his team had played for a draw against Algeria after Saturday's 3-3 World Cup match sent both teams through to the knockout stage. Austria will now face off against Spain in the round of 32 on July 2 in California.

events.title: Heatwave spreads across eastern Europe, France records 1,000 deaths in a week
events.summary: The record-breaking heatwave in France is set to come to an end on Sunday, with the country's extreme weather alert expected to be lifted by the evening, bringing relief after temperatures climbed as high as 40°C this week. But the heatwave is far from over, as it shifts eastward, with Germany and the Czech Republic now preparing for the worst of the extreme weather.

events.title: Key poll in New Caledonia: First major vote on French Pacific islands since 2024 riots
events.summary: Voting in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia closed on Sunday, with a turnout of 54.42 per cent, as citizens participated in the archipelago's first provincial elections since 2019. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue. Political scientist and post-colonial activist, Françoise Vergès, joins us for further insights.

events.title: More than 1,300 excess deaths linked to record-breaking Europe heatwave, WHO says
events.summary: More than 1,300 excess deaths have been recorded in connection with Europe's record-breaking heatwave since June 21, according to the World Health Organization on Sunday. Earlier that day, health officials in France reported that there had been around 1,000 more deaths than expected in the country since Wednesday.

events.title: What France gets wrong about air conditioning
events.summary: Is this the heatwave that finally changes French minds on air conditioning? Not the mind of environment minister Monique Barbut, for one, who said she was "horrified" by the idea of it becoming widespread. The rest of the world looked on perplexed as France suffered a brutal week, particularly for schools, hospitals and care homes. Long suspicious of it, French people are slowly adopting A/C, but it remains politically controversial and difficult to install in historic towns and cities.

events.title: The week in pictures: Earthquakes in Venezuela, Starmer's resignation and extreme heat in France
events.summary: The resignation of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer less than two years after his election victory, extreme heat grips France, fans fete the World Cup and twin earthquakes ravage Venezuela: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.title: World Cup 2026 : 215 goals in record breaking group stage
events.summary: Austria and Algeria went down to the wire in a match full of drama. Austria's last-minute goal means both teams are through to the next round. Celebrations are also due for the Democratic Republic of Congo who have qualified for the first time in their history, thanks to their 3-1 win against Uzbekistan. Portugal and Colombia stayed level at 0-0. England finished top of group L, putting two past Panama. The round of 32 fixtures are all confirmed, the first knockout game sees Canada go up against South Africa in Los Angeles.

events.title: Albania's "Flamingo revolution": Trump-linked luxury resort plan triggers mass protests
events.summary: Protests in Albania entered their fifth week on Saturday, with demonstrations that began in May over a planned luxury resort project linked to the Trump family. Since then, the movement has expanded into a broader criticism of Prime Minister Edi Rama and his government, with protesters raising wider concerns about governance and political accountability. Vladimir Karaj, a journalist and editor at the Balkan Investigative Reporting Network, joins us from Tirana for further insights.

events.title: Serbia’s Vučić to resign, opening path to early elections amid political tensions
events.summary: Serbia’s President Aleksandar Vučić has announced that he will resign within weeks, paving the way for early elections. There is speculation that the populist leader could attempt to move into the more powerful role of Prime Minister. The announcement comes after more than a year of heightened tensions in the Balkan nation, where university students have led protests demanding political change.

events.title: New Caledonia election tests territory's future after unrest and deadlock
events.summary: New Caledonia goes to the poll on Sunday in crucial elections. The possibility of independence from France remains the central political issue, with today’s vote taking place peacefully. The election comes two years after plans to expand voting rights to thousands of non-Indigenous residents triggered deadly unrest and forced the postponement of elections in 2024. Eliza Herbert reports.

events.title: New US-Iran exchange of attacks threatens ceasefire
events.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard on Sunday launched attacks targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US air strikes on 10 Iranian sites. The Islamic Republic threatened a “complete halt” to negotiations to end the war if Washington continues its attacks. Efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz without Iran's direct oversight sparked the crossfire now gripping the region. France 24 correspondent Reza Sayah tells the latest from Tehran.

events.title: DR Congo beat Uzbekistan and fly to World Cup round of 32
events.summary: Congo defeated Uzbekistan 3–1, securing a place in the World Cup knockout stage. The victory means nine of the tournament's ten African teams have advanced to the next round, joining South Africa, Morocco, Senegal, Ivory Coast, Cape Verde, Egypt, Algeria and Ghana. For some teams, including Congo, it marks the first time they have reached this stage of the competition.

events.title: Venezuela death toll reaches 1,430
events.summary: More than three days after Venezuela was struck by its most powerful earthquake in over a century, hopes of finding survivors are fading. Experts say the first 72 hours are critical for rescues, after which the operation increasingly becomes one of recovering bodies. The confirmed death toll has climbed to 1,430 and continues to rise by the hundreds, while tens of thousands of people remain missing. The UN's migration agency estimates that nearly seven million people were affected by Wednesday's twin earthquakes and are in need of shelter, clean water and other essential aid. Yinka Oyetade reports.

events.title: New Caledonia polls close in French territory's first provincial elections since 2019
events.summary: Voting in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia closed on Sunday, with a turnout of 54.42 per cent, as citizens participated in the archipelago's first provincial elections since 2019. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia secure top spot ahead of disappointing Portugal
events.summary: Portugal needed to beat Colombia to reclaim first place in Group K. But in the end, it was Colombia who came closest to victory. The two teams ultimately drew.

events.title: Saudi Arabian helicopter crash kills 14
events.summary: A helicopter crash in Ras Tanura, on Saudi Arabia's eastern coast, has killed 14 people.

events.title: Who will face who in last 32 of World Cup?
Thirteen injured and pilot killed as plane hits Beijing's tallest building
events.summary: Thirteen people were injured and the pilot was killed when a light aircraft crashed into a Beijing skyscraper on Friday.

events.title: Iraqi officials, including lawmakers, arrested on corruption charges
events.summary: Iraqi political officials have been arrested on corruption charges

events.title: Pakistan's military says 3 soldiers killed in militant attack
events.summary: Pakistan’s military says three paramilitary soldiers were killed and four others wounded in fighting after militants launched an overnight attack on a Rangers regional headquarters in the southern port city of Karachi

events.title: Ugandan army chief orders the closure of a major news platform
events.summary: Uganda's military chief and son of President Yoweri Museveni has ordered the shutdown of a major news organization
